steps.title: Use context around conceivable
steps.content: Seacole says nature threw every conceivable obstacle in the way of the journey. In this context, the word describes the full range of obstacles that could be imagined.

steps.title: Match imagined with possible
steps.content: Choice B means ordinary, Choice C means able to be seen, and Choice D means simple or fundamental. The context calls for a word meaning able to be imagined or possible. The answer is A.
